courseleaf.title: History, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The minor in History consists of five upper division courses chosen so that at least three courses are in one field and at least one course is in another field. The two fields shall be chosen from among those defined in the catalog for the major. However, students may also, in consultation with and with the authorization of a faculty advisor, define other thematic fields.

Minor Advisor

For updated information, contact the Undergraduate Advisor.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Three courses in one concentration.12
See major concentrations: Africa, Asia, Europe, Latin America, Middle East, United States, & World.
Additional units to reach 20.8
Total Units20
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The History minor equips students with the essential skills and basic knowledge necessary to navigate the modern world as well as a career in most professions. Through the study of past events and societies, students learn to read critically, debate issues and historical context substantively, and write with clarity.
